revision.text:
{{Short description|Archaeological site in Futaba, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kiyotosaku Cave Tombs | native_name = 清戸迫横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = Kiyotosaku aero 1975.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of location in 1975 | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kiyotosaku Cave Tombs | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|26|31|N|141|00|17|E|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Futaba, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1988 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiyotosaku Cave Tombs'''|清戸迫横穴| Kiyotosaku yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing numerous [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=Cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the town of [[Futaba, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is tomb No.76 is a highly [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns, human figures and animals painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/2/ |script-title=ja:清戸さく76号横穴 |trans-title=Kiyodosaku No. 76 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012}}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204833 |title= 清戸迫横穴|trans-title=Kiyotosaku yokoaka|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site dates from the first half of the 7th century and consists of over 300 graves located on hilly land. The existence of these tombs has been known since ancient times, but they were only excavated in 1968, as part of [[rescue archaeology]] during construction work on the Futaba Minami Elementary School. Most were destroyed by the construction; however, tomb No.76 was found to contain well preserved mural paintings. The tomb has a rectangular opening facing southeast and is 3.15 meters in length, 1.56 meters in height and 2.34 to 2.8 meters in width. The murals include spiral designed in red iron oxide, with a crowned human figure wearing a ''[[hakama]]'' and shoes, and another figure with a helmet and a raised left hand next to a horse. Other designs include figures with bows and arrows representing a hunting scene, with wild boar, deer and dogs.
The site was damaged by the [[2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] and since the [[Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster]] has been in an area off limits to the public due to radiation. In February 2017, [[Tohoku University]] received special permission to revisit the site, and to make a detailed three-dimensional scan of the interior of the tomb, in order to have a detailed record of the dimensions of the tomb and its interior decorations just in case future damage should occur. <ref name="tohoku">{{cite web|url=https://www.tohoku.ac.jp/japanese/2017/02/news20170215-01.html|website=tohoku.ac.jp|title=国史跡清戸迫横穴の3次元計測を行いました | ニュース | 東北大学 -Tohoku University- |accessdate=22 December 2018}}</ref>
The site is about 20 minutes on foot from [[Futaba Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://futabadaruma.jp Futaba Tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Archaeological site in Futaba, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kiyotosaku Cave Tombs | native_name = 清戸迫横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = Kiyotosaku aero 1975.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of location in 1975 | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kiyotosaku Cave Tombs | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|26|31|N|141|00|17|E|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Futaba, Fukushima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1988 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiyotosaku Cave Tombs'''|清戸迫横穴| Kiyotosaku yokoaka}} is an [[archaeological site]] containing numerous [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=Cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the town of [[Futaba, Fukushima]] in the southern [[Tōhoku region]] of [[Japan]]. It is significant in that it is tomb No.76 is a highly [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns, human figures and animals painted in red on the walls of the [[burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/2/ |script-title=ja:清戸さく76号横穴 |trans-title=Kiyodosaku No. 76 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012}}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204833 |title= 清戸迫横穴|trans-title=Kiyotosaku yokoaka|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site dates from the first half of the 7th century and consists of over 300 graves located on hilly land. The existence of these tombs has been known since ancient times, but they were only excavated in 1968, as part of [[rescue archaeology]] during construction work on the Futaba Minami Elementary School. Most were destroyed by the construction; however, tomb No.76 was found to contain well preserved mural paintings. The tomb has a rectangular opening facing southeast and is 3.15 meters in length, 1.56 meters in height and 2.34 to 2.8 meters in width. The murals include spiral designed in red iron oxide, with a crowned human figure wearing a ''[[hakama]]'' and shoes, and another figure with a helmet and a raised left hand next to a horse. Other designs include figures with bows and arrows representing a hunting scene, with wild boar, deer and dogs.
The site was damaged by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] and since the [[Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster]] has been in an area off limits to the public due to radiation. In February 2017, [[Tohoku University]] received special permission to revisit the site, and to make a detailed three-dimensional scan of the interior of the tomb, in order to have a detailed record of the dimensions of the tomb and its interior decorations just in case future damage should occur. <ref name="tohoku">{{cite web|url=https://www.tohoku.ac.jp/japanese/2017/02/news20170215-01.html|website=tohoku.ac.jp|title=国史跡清戸迫横穴の3次元計測を行いました | ニュース | 東北大学 -Tohoku University- |accessdate=22 December 2018}}</ref>
The site is about 20 minutes on foot from [[Futaba Station]] on the [[JR East]] [[Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://futabadaruma.jp Futaba Tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The site is about 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:Futaba Station|Futaba Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].

tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Futaba, Fukushima|Futaba, Fukushima]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is significant in that it is tomb No.76 is a highly [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns, human figures and animals painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/2/ |script-title=ja:清戸さく76号横穴 |trans-title=Kiyodosaku No. 76 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012}}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204833 |title= 清戸迫横穴|trans-title=Kiyotosaku yokoaka|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site dates from the first half of the 7th century and consists of over 300 graves located on hilly land. The existence of these tombs has been known since ancient times, but they were only excavated in 1968, as part of [[:en:Rescue archaeology|Rescue archaeology]] during construction work on the Futaba Minami Elementary School. Most were destroyed by the construction; however, tomb No.76 was found to contain well preserved mural paintings. The tomb has a rectangular opening facing southeast and is 3.15 meters in length, 1.56 meters in height and 2.34 to 2.8 meters in width. The murals include spiral designed in red iron oxide, with a crowned human figure wearing a ''[[:en:Hakama|Hakama]]'' and shoes, and another figure with a helmet and a raised left hand next to a horse. Other designs include figures with bows and arrows representing a hunting scene, with wild boar, deer and dogs.
The site was damaged by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] and since the [[:en:Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster|Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster]] has been in an area off limits to the public due to radiation. In February 2017, [[:en:Tohoku University|Tohoku University]] received special permission to revisit the site, and to make a detailed three-dimensional scan of the interior of the tomb, in order to have a detailed record of the dimensions of the tomb and its interior decorations just in case future damage should occur. <ref name="tohoku">{{cite web|url=https://www.tohoku.ac.jp/japanese/2017/02/news20170215-01.html|website=tohoku.ac.jp|title=国史跡清戸迫横穴の3次元計測を行いました | ニュース | 東北大学 -Tohoku University- |accessdate=22 December 2018}}</ref>
The site is about 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:Futaba Station|Futaba Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].

of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiyotosaku Cave Tombs'''|清戸迫横穴| Kiyotosaku yokoaka}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] containing numerous [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=Cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Futaba, Fukushima|Futaba, Fukushima]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is significant in that it is tomb No.76 is a highly [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns, human figures and animals painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/2/ |script-title=ja:清戸さく76号横穴 |trans-title=Kiyodosaku No. 76 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012}}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204833 |title= 清戸迫横穴|trans-title=Kiyotosaku yokoaka|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site dates from the first half of the 7th century and consists of over 300 graves located on hilly land. The existence of these tombs has been known since ancient times, but they were only excavated in 1968, as part of [[:en:Rescue archaeology|Rescue archaeology]] during construction work on the Futaba Minami Elementary School. Most were destroyed by the construction; however, tomb No.76 was found to contain well preserved mural paintings. The tomb has a rectangular opening facing southeast and is 3.15 meters in length, 1.56 meters in height and 2.34 to 2.8 meters in width. The murals include spiral designed in red iron oxide, with a crowned human figure wearing a ''[[:en:Hakama|Hakama]]'' and shoes, and another figure with a helmet and a raised left hand next to a horse. Other designs include figures with bows and arrows representing a hunting scene, with wild boar, deer and dogs.
The site was damaged by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] and since the [[:en:Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster|Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster]] has been in an area off limits to the public due to radiation. In February 2017, [[:en:Tohoku University|Tohoku University]] received special permission to revisit the site, and to make a detailed three-dimensional scan of the interior of the tomb, in order to have a detailed record of the dimensions of the tomb and its interior decorations just in case future damage should occur. <ref name="tohoku">{{cite web|url=https://www.tohoku.ac.jp/japanese/2017/02/news20170215-01.html|website=tohoku.ac.jp|title=国史跡清戸迫横穴の3次元計測を行いました | ニュース | 東北大学 -Tohoku University- |accessdate=22 December 2018}}</ref>
The site is about 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:Futaba Station|Futaba Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].

Most were destroyed by the construction; however, tomb No.76 was found to contain well preserved mural paintings. The tomb has a rectangular opening facing southeast and is 3.15 meters in length, 1.56 meters in height and 2.34 to 2.8 meters in width. The murals include spiral designed in red iron oxide, with a crowned human figure wearing a ''[[:en:Hakama|Hakama]]'' and shoes, and another figure with a helmet and a raised left hand next to a horse. Other designs include figures with bows and arrows representing a hunting scene, with wild boar, deer and dogs.
The site was damaged by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] and since the [[:en:Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster|Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster]] has been in an area off limits to the public due to radiation. In February 2017, [[:en:Tohoku University|Tohoku University]] received special permission to revisit the site, and to make a detailed three-dimensional scan of the interior of the tomb, in order to have a detailed record of the dimensions of the tomb and its interior decorations just in case future damage should occur. <ref name="tohoku">{{cite web|url=https://www.tohoku.ac.jp/japanese/2017/02/news20170215-01.html|website=tohoku.ac.jp|title=国史跡清戸迫横穴の3次元計測を行いました | ニュース | 東北大学 -Tohoku University- |accessdate=22 December 2018}}</ref>
The site is about 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:Futaba Station|Futaba Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].

Tombs]][[ja:清戸迫横穴]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11560641]] Wikidata name: Kiyotosaku Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11560641]] Wikidata name: Kiyotosaku Cave Tombs [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Archaeological site in Futaba, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kiyotosaku Cave Tombs | native_name = 清戸迫横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = Kiyotosaku aero 1975.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of location in 1975 | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kiyotosaku Cave Tombs | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|26|31|N|141|00|17|E|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Futaba, Fukushima|Futaba, Fukushima]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1988 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiyotosaku Cave Tombs'''|清戸迫横穴| Kiyotosaku yokoaka}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] containing numerous [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=Cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Futaba, Fukushima|Futaba, Fukushima]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is significant in that it is tomb No.76 is a highly [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns, human figures and animals painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/2/ |script-title=ja:清戸さく76号横穴 |trans-title=Kiyodosaku No. 76 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012}}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204833 |title= 清戸迫横穴|trans-title=Kiyotosaku yokoaka|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site dates from the first half of the 7th century and consists of over 300 graves located on hilly land. The existence of these tombs has been known since ancient times, but they were only excavated in 1968, as part of [[:en:Rescue archaeology|Rescue archaeology]] during construction work on the Futaba Minami Elementary School. Most were destroyed by the construction; however, tomb No.76 was found to contain well preserved mural paintings. The tomb has a rectangular opening facing southeast and is 3.15 meters in length, 1.56 meters in height and 2.34 to 2.8 meters in width. The murals include spiral designed in red iron oxide, with a crowned human figure wearing a ''[[:en:Hakama|Hakama]]'' and shoes, and another figure with a helmet and a raised left hand next to a horse. Other designs include figures with bows and arrows representing a hunting scene, with wild boar, deer and dogs.
The site was damaged by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] and since the [[:en:Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster|Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster]] has been in an area off limits to the public due to radiation. In February 2017, [[:en:Tohoku University|Tohoku University]] received special permission to revisit the site, and to make a detailed three-dimensional scan of the interior of the tomb, in order to have a detailed record of the dimensions of the tomb and its interior decorations just in case future damage should occur. <ref name="tohoku">{{cite web|url=https://www.tohoku.ac.jp/japanese/2017/02/news20170215-01.html|website=tohoku.ac.jp|title=国史跡清戸迫横穴の3次元計測を行いました | ニュース | 東北大学 -Tohoku University- |accessdate=22 December 2018}}</ref>
The site is about 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:Futaba Station|Futaba Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://futabadaruma.jp Futaba Tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}

architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiyotosaku Cave Tombs'''|清戸迫横穴| Kiyotosaku yokoaka}} is an [[:en:Archaeological site|Archaeological site]] containing numerous [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=Cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the town of [[:en:Futaba, Fukushima|Futaba, Fukushima]] in the southern [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It is significant in that it is tomb No.76 is a highly [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns, human figures and animals painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/2/ |script-title=ja:清戸さく76号横穴 |trans-title=Kiyodosaku No. 76 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012}}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204833 |title= 清戸迫横穴|trans-title=Kiyotosaku yokoaka|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site dates from the first half of the 7th century and consists of over 300 graves located on hilly land. The existence of these tombs has been known since ancient times, but they were only excavated in 1968, as part of [[:en:Rescue archaeology|Rescue archaeology]] during construction work on the Futaba Minami Elementary School. Most were destroyed by the construction; however, tomb No.76 was found to contain well preserved mural paintings. The tomb has a rectangular opening facing southeast and is 3.15 meters in length, 1.56 meters in height and 2.34 to 2.8 meters in width. The murals include spiral designed in red iron oxide, with a crowned human figure wearing a ''[[:en:Hakama|Hakama]]'' and shoes, and another figure with a helmet and a raised left hand next to a horse. Other designs include figures with bows and arrows representing a hunting scene, with wild boar, deer and dogs.
The site was damaged by the [[:en:2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]] and since the [[:en:Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster|Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster]] has been in an area off limits to the public due to radiation. In February 2017, [[:en:Tohoku University|Tohoku University]] received special permission to revisit the site, and to make a detailed three-dimensional scan of the interior of the tomb, in order to have a detailed record of the dimensions of the tomb and its interior decorations just in case future damage should occur. <ref name="tohoku">{{cite web|url=https://www.tohoku.ac.jp/japanese/2017/02/news20170215-01.html|website=tohoku.ac.jp|title=国史跡清戸迫横穴の3次元計測を行いました | ニュース | 東北大学 -Tohoku University- |accessdate=22 December 2018}}</ref>
The site is about 20 minutes on foot from [[:en:Futaba Station|Futaba Station]] on the [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|East Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Jōban Line|Jōban Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)]]

Fukushima}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1988 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiyotosaku Cave Tombs'''|清戸迫横穴| Kiyotosaku yokoaka}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site}} containing numerous [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=Cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the town of {{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|en|Futaba, Fukushima|WD=Q1023949|lt=Futaba, Fukushima}} in the southern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is significant in that it is tomb No.76 is a highly [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns, human figures and animals painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/2/ |script-title=ja:清戸さく76号横穴 |trans-title=Kiyodosaku No. 76 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012}}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204833 |title= 清戸迫横穴|trans-title=Kiyotosaku yokoaka|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site dates from the first half of the 7th century and consists of over 300 graves located on hilly land. The existence of these tombs has been known since ancient times, but they were only excavated in 1968, as part of {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|WD=Q653838|lt=Rescue archaeology}} during construction work on the Futaba Minami Elementary School. Most were destroyed by the construction; however, tomb No.76 was found to contain well preserved mural paintings. The tomb has a rectangular opening facing southeast and is 3.15 meters in length, 1.56 meters in height and 2.34 to 2.8 meters in width. The murals include spiral designed in red iron oxide, with a crowned human figure wearing a ''{{ill|Hakama|en|Hakama|WD=Q164668|lt=Hakama}}'' and shoes, and another figure with a helmet and a raised left hand next to a horse. Other designs include figures with bows and arrows representing a hunting scene, with wild boar, deer and dogs.
The site was damaged by the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q36204|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}} and since the [[:en:Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster|Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster]] has been in an area off limits to the public due to radiation. In February 2017, {{ill|Tohoku University|en|Tohoku University|WD=Q1062129|lt=Tohoku University}} received special permission to revisit the site, and to make a detailed three-dimensional scan of the interior of the tomb, in order to have a detailed record of the dimensions of the tomb and its interior decorations just in case future damage should occur. <ref name="tohoku">{{cite web|url=https://www.tohoku.ac.jp/japanese/2017/02/news20170215-01.html|website=tohoku.ac.jp|title=国史跡清戸迫横穴の3次元計測を行いました | ニュース | 東北大学 -Tohoku University- |accessdate=22 December 2018}}</ref>
The site is about 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Futaba Station|en|Futaba Station|WD=Q5510442|lt=Futaba Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|WD=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|WD=Q6573260|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://futabadaruma.jp Futaba Tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Archaeological site in Futaba, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kiyotosaku Cave Tombs | native_name = 清戸迫横穴 | native_name_lang = | image = Kiyotosaku aero 1975.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of location in 1975 | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kiyotosaku Cave Tombs | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|26|31|N|141|00|17|E|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|en|Futaba, Fukushima|WD=Q1023949|lt=Futaba, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1988 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiyotosaku Cave Tombs'''|清戸迫横穴| Kiyotosaku yokoaka}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site}} containing numerous [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=Cave Tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Cave Tomb|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Cave Tomb|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the town of {{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|en|Futaba, Fukushima|WD=Q1023949|lt=Futaba, Fukushima}} in the southern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is significant in that it is tomb No.76 is a highly [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns, human figures and animals painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/2/ |script-title=ja:清戸さく76号横穴 |trans-title=Kiyodosaku No. 76 Cave Tomb |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012}}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204833 |title= 清戸迫横穴|trans-title=Kiyotosaku yokoaka|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site dates from the first half of the 7th century and consists of over 300 graves located on hilly land. The existence of these tombs has been known since ancient times, but they were only excavated in 1968, as part of {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|WD=Q653838|lt=Rescue archaeology}} during construction work on the Futaba Minami Elementary School. Most were destroyed by the construction; however, tomb No.76 was found to contain well preserved mural paintings. The tomb has a rectangular opening facing southeast and is 3.15 meters in length, 1.56 meters in height and 2.34 to 2.8 meters in width. The murals include spiral designed in red iron oxide, with a crowned human figure wearing a ''{{ill|Hakama|en|Hakama|WD=Q164668|lt=Hakama}}'' and shoes, and another figure with a helmet and a raised left hand next to a horse. Other designs include figures with bows and arrows representing a hunting scene, with wild boar, deer and dogs.
The site was damaged by the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q36204|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}} and since the [[:en:Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster|Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster]] has been in an area off limits to the public due to radiation. In February 2017, {{ill|Tohoku University|en|Tohoku University|WD=Q1062129|lt=Tohoku University}} received special permission to revisit the site, and to make a detailed three-dimensional scan of the interior of the tomb, in order to have a detailed record of the dimensions of the tomb and its interior decorations just in case future damage should occur. <ref name="tohoku">{{cite web|url=https://www.tohoku.ac.jp/japanese/2017/02/news20170215-01.html|website=tohoku.ac.jp|title=国史跡清戸迫横穴の3次元計測を行いました | ニュース | 東北大学 -Tohoku University- |accessdate=22 December 2018}}</ref>
The site is about 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Futaba Station|en|Futaba Station|WD=Q5510442|lt=Futaba Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|WD=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|WD=Q6573260|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://futabadaruma.jp Futaba Tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}

image_size = | alt = | caption = Aerial photo of location in 1975 | map_type = Japan Fukushima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kiyotosaku Yokoanabos | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|37|26|31|N|141|00|17|E|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|en|Futaba, Fukushima|WD=Q1023949|lt=Futaba, Fukushima|1=Futaba, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} | type = cave tomb | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = [[Kofun period]] | abandoned = | epochs = | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1988 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} {{nihongo|'''Kiyotosaku Yokoanabos'''|清戸迫横穴| Kiyotosaku yokoaka}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|WD=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} containing numerous [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=Yokoanabo|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in what is now part of the town of {{ill|Futaba, Fukushima|en|Futaba, Fukushima|WD=Q1023949|lt=Futaba, Fukushima|1=Futaba, Fukushima}} in the southern {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is significant in that it is tomb No.76 is a highly [[decorated kofun]] with geometric patterns, human figures and animals painted in red on the walls of the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]].<ref>{{cite web |url=http://kyuhaku.jmc.or.jp/d_frame.php/7/2/ |script-title=ja:清戸さく76号横穴 |trans-title=Kiyodosaku No. 76 Yokoanabo |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Kyushu National Museum]] |accessdate=25 August 2012}}</ref> The site was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1968.<ref>{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204833 |title= 清戸迫横穴|trans-title=Kiyotosaku yokoaka|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The site dates from the first half of the 7th century and consists of over 300 graves located on hilly land. The existence of these tombs has been known since ancient times, but they were only excavated in 1968, as part of {{ill|Rescue archaeology|en|Rescue archaeology|WD=Q653838|lt=Rescue archaeology|1=Rescue archaeology}} during construction work on the Futaba Minami Elementary School. Most were destroyed by the construction; however, tomb No.76 was found to contain well preserved mural paintings. The tomb has a rectangular opening facing southeast and is 3.15 meters in length, 1.56 meters in height and 2.34 to 2.8 meters in width. The murals include spiral designed in red iron oxide, with a crowned human figure wearing a ''{{ill|Hakama|en|Hakama|WD=Q164668|lt=Hakama|1=Hakama}}'' and shoes, and another figure with a helmet and a raised left hand next to a horse. Other designs include figures with bows and arrows representing a hunting scene, with wild boar, deer and dogs.
The site was damaged by the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q36204|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|1=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}} and since the [[:en:Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster|Fukushima Daiichi nuclear disaster]] has been in an area off limits to the public due to radiation. In February 2017, {{ill|Tohoku University|en|Tohoku University|WD=Q1062129|lt=Tohoku University|1=Tohoku University}} received special permission to revisit the site, and to make a detailed three-dimensional scan of the interior of the tomb, in order to have a detailed record of the dimensions of the tomb and its interior decorations just in case future damage should occur. <ref name="tohoku">{{cite web|url=https://www.tohoku.ac.jp/japanese/2017/02/news20170215-01.html|website=tohoku.ac.jp|title=国史跡清戸迫横穴の3次元計測を行いました | ニュース | 東北大学 -Tohoku University- |accessdate=22 December 2018}}</ref>
The site is about 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|Futaba Station|en|Futaba Station|WD=Q5510442|lt=Futaba Station|1=Futaba Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Jōban Line|en|Jōban Line|WD=Q1369263|lt=Jōban Line|1=Jōban Line}}.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|WD=Q6573260|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[http://futabadaruma.jp Futaba Tourist information home page] {{in lang|ja}}
